Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for the AM vendor exposure reports. Use conversations_search_messages with queries related to "rate increase" or "vendor" or "exposure" or "Heartland" or "Swift." David posted a request in #customer-engagement (April 16) asking each AM to identify which active clients are most exposed to the vendor rate increases. Mina replied (April 16) identifying Vectral Systems and BrightLoop Analytics as the top-risk accounts — Vectral for churn/trust sensitivity, BrightLoop for volume sensitivity. Emeka replied (April 16) flagging Mosaic Robotics as exposed due to specialized handling needs and tight timelines on active relocations. David followed up (April 17) noting that Catalina had still not responded, leaving a gap in Midwest account coverage. The agent should also discover broader Slack discussion about the vendor rate increases from Chloe, Marcus, and others.
OE 2: Search Slack and email for information about the vendor consolidation crisis -- specifically the Heartland 20% rate increase effective July 1 (tied to the unresolved Q1 overbilling dispute, per Jake Loomis's formal notice) and the Swift 15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment (effective immediately on all new bookings, per Greg Pallone's pricing update). Look for messages from Chloe Vance, Marcus Thorne, and Alejandro Fuentes discussing vendor rate impacts. The agent should discover that Alejandro began modeling margin exposure from the vendor rate increases but noted the inputs across three systems (Airtable, QuickBooks, CRM) do not reconcile cleanly into a defensible client-by-client forecast, that Chloe probed backup vendor Lakeline Logistics but they only cover Midwest and Southeast lanes -- not a national replacement plan -- per Marcus's vendor strategy working thread (Slack, April 25) informed by Chloe's Lakeline investigation, and that Elena ordered a formal vendor strategy memo by Wednesday and assigned Marcus as owner.
OE 3: Query Airtable relocation records to build the real exposure map -- identify which active/in-progress and recent relocations used Heartland Movers or Swift Relocations as vendors, and map them to client accounts. Use airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on the Relocations table (tblRelocations01, base appMoveOpsOps001) to find all relocation records and search the "Special Requirements" free-text field for vendor assignment information -- some records contain structured notes like "Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers" or "Moving Vendor: Swift Relocations" embedded within this field, while other records may not include explicit vendor assignments. Cross-reference this with the Client Accounts table to identify which AM manages each exposed account. The agent should discover the vendor assignments across relocations for Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, and Mosaic Robotics via the "Moving Vendor:" field in Airtable Special Requirements. Note: NorthWind Technologies does NOT have "Moving Vendor:" entries in its Airtable relocation records, but its vendor exposure can be found in the Airtable Special Requirements prose -- Emilia Cruz's record mentions "Heartland for household goods" and Raj Patel's record mentions "confirm Swift will accept supervised loading protocol" -- as well as in CRM deal descriptions (which explicitly reference "Heartland (household goods)" for Emilia and "via Swift Relocations" for Raj). The vendor information is present in Airtable but not in the structured "Moving Vendor:" label format used by other companies. More broadly, vendor information is only explicitly stated in Special Requirements for a subset of relocation records; the exposure map may need supplementing from other sources to fill gaps.
OE 4: Search Slack and email for updates on the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap situation. Use conversations_search_messages and/or search_emails with queries related to "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "nonprofit pricing." The agent should discover:
- Hana Kim accidentally swapped the invoice PDF attachments, sending Sunbelt the Palmetto nonprofit-rate invoice and vice versa
- Sunbelt's Raj Greenfield saw the nonprofit pricing, emailed Emeka for clarification, and when Emeka didn't respond clearly, escalated directly to David
- Palmetto's Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged the inflated invoice as an audit risk
- Emeka went quiet for days before eventually reconstructing the error and sending corrective email replies to both clients explaining the billing error, with promises to provide the corrected PDF copies through finance -- but not yet attaching the correct PDFs at this point (corrective emails sent April 22; Slack reconstruction posted April 23) -- note that the QuickBooks invoices (INV-2026-0398 for Sunbelt at $4,800 and INV-2026-0399 for Palmetto at $2,400) were correct all along; the failure was in the outbound email attachment layer, not in billing
- Marcus framed it as revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk (Slack, April 16)
- Marcus put a hard line under the incident and issued cross-functional EOD action items for Finance, Customer Engagement, and Engineering
OE 5: Check the CRM deal records for Sunbelt Venture Partners and Palmetto Community Foundation to see whether the deals reflect the risk from the invoice swap. Use crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries for "Sunbelt" and "Palmetto." The agent should check whether the deal stage or deal notes have been updated to reflect the at-risk status caused by the pricing exposure. Based on universe data, Sunbelt and Palmetto deals may or may not have been updated to reflect this risk -- the agent needs to verify and potentially flag this gap.
OE 6: Check the CRM deal records for Catalina's accounts -- NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, and PivotPoint Labs -- to see if they reflect vendor rate increase risk. Use crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries for "NorthWind," "Canopy," and "PivotPoint." The agent should also check Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics, and other accounts flagged by Mina and Emeka to verify whether those deal records reflect the vendor exposure. The agent should discover that many deal records likely do not reflect the current risk, confirming David's concern.
OE 7: Look up contact details for Catalina Dubois, David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails. The agent needs:
- Catalina's email: catalina.dubois@moveops.com (recipient of the escalation email)
- David's email: david.chen@moveops.com (recipient of the summary email)
- Marcus's and Chloe's details for scheduling the meeting
OE 8: Send an email (via send_email) to Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) from David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) laying out the vendor rate increase situation, the stakes for her three accounts (NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs), and what David needs from her -- specifically her assessment of which of her clients' relocations use Heartland or Swift and how the rate increases affect those accounts. The email should reference that Marcus's vendor strategy memo for Elena will have a gap without her input, and that Mina and Emeka have already responded.
OE 9: Check calendar availability for David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Chloe Vance using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (for the week of April 27-May 2, 2026) to find a suitable time slot. The prompt explicitly requests: "I also need to sit down with Marcus and Chloe sometime next week."
OE 10: Schedule a meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event for an available slot during the week of April 27 – May 2, 2026. The meeting should relate to aligning the vendor response from finance, operations, and customer engagement perspectives. Attendees should include:
- David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com)
- Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com)
- Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com)
OE 11: Send an email (via send_email) from David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) to David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) with a comprehensive summary of which client accounts are exposed to the Heartland/Swift rate increases and why. The prompt explicitly says "email me the summary of which accounts are exposed and why so I can share it with my team." The summary should cover:
- Mina's findings: Vectral Systems and BrightLoop Analytics are most exposed
- Emeka's findings: Mosaic Robotics is exposed
- Catalina's accounts (NorthWind, Canopy, PivotPoint): exposure status based on Airtable relocation data showing which vendors are assigned to their moves
- The Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap situation: current status of resolution, what Emeka communicated, and whether deal records reflect the risk
- The overall context: dual rate increases (Heartland 20% effective July 1 per Jake Loomis's formal notice, Swift 15% immediate base rate increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge per Greg Pallone's pricing update), Alejandro's margin exposure modeling effort (noting inputs across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM do not yet reconcile into a defensible forecast), and the gap in Catalina's reporting
OE 12: Update CRM records for all accounts exposed to risk, using crm_create_engagement (with risk context in the body) or crm_update_deal_stage (with a risk-related stage name). This is an implicit requirement -- David says "half our deal records don't reflect the risk yet" and "if someone touches one of these deals tomorrow they won't know any of this is happening." The agent should update records for:
- All seven accounts exposed to vendor rate increases: Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics, NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs, and GreenStack Energy. Each update should reference the vendor rate exposure affecting that account.
- Both accounts affected by the invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners and Palmetto Community Foundation. Each update should reference the invoice swap incident and pricing exposure.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for the AM vendor exposure reports. Use conversations_search_messages with queries related to "rate increase" or "vendor" or "exposure" or "Heartland" or "Swift." David posted a request in #customer-engagement (April 16) asking each AM to identify which active clients are most exposed to the vendor rate increases. Mina replied (April 16) identifying Vectral Systems and BrightLoop Analytics as the top-risk accounts — Vectral for churn/trust sensitivity, BrightLoop for volume sensitivity. Emeka replied (April 16) flagging Mosaic Robotics as exposed due to specialized handling needs and tight timelines on active relocations. David followed up (April 17) noting that Catalina had still not responded, leaving a gap in Midwest account coverage. The agent should also discover broader Slack discussion about the vendor rate increases from Chloe, Marcus, and others.
OE 2: Search Slack and email for information about the vendor consolidation crisis -- specifically the Heartland 20% rate increase effective July 1 (tied to the unresolved Q1 overbilling dispute, per Jake Loomis's formal notice) and the Swift 15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment (effective immediately on all new bookings, per Greg Pallone's pricing update). Look for messages from Chloe Vance, Marcus Thorne, and Alejandro Fuentes discussing vendor rate impacts. The agent should discover that Alejandro began modeling margin exposure from the vendor rate increases but noted the inputs across three systems (Airtable, QuickBooks, CRM) do not reconcile cleanly into a defensible client-by-client forecast, that Chloe probed backup vendor Lakeline Logistics but they only cover Midwest and Southeast lanes -- not a national replacement plan -- per Marcus's vendor strategy working thread (Slack, April 25) informed by Chloe's Lakeline investigation, and that Elena ordered a formal vendor strategy memo by Wednesday and assigned Marcus as owner.
OE 3: Query Airtable relocation records to build the real exposure map -- identify which active/in-progress and recent relocations used Heartland Movers or Swift Relocations as vendors, and map them to client accounts. Use airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on the Relocations table (tblRelocations01, base appMoveOpsOps001) to find all relocation records and search the "Special Requirements" free-text field for vendor assignment information -- some records contain structured notes like "Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers" or "Moving Vendor: Swift Relocations" embedded within this field, while other records may not include explicit vendor assignments. Cross-reference this with the Client Accounts table to identify which AM manages each exposed account. The agent should discover the vendor assignments across relocations for Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, and Mosaic Robotics via the "Moving Vendor:" field in Airtable Special Requirements. Note: NorthWind Technologies does NOT have "Moving Vendor:" entries in its Airtable relocation records, but its vendor exposure can be found in the Airtable Special Requirements prose -- Emilia Cruz's record mentions "Heartland for household goods" and Raj Patel's record mentions "confirm Swift will accept supervised loading protocol" -- as well as in CRM deal descriptions (which explicitly reference "Heartland (household goods)" for Emilia and "via Swift Relocations" for Raj). The vendor information is present in Airtable but not in the structured "Moving Vendor:" label format used by other companies. More broadly, vendor information is only explicitly stated in Special Requirements for a subset of relocation records; the exposure map may need supplementing from other sources to fill gaps.
OE 4: Search Slack and email for updates on the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap situation. Use conversations_search_messages and/or search_emails with queries related to "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "nonprofit pricing." The agent should discover:
- Hana Kim accidentally swapped the invoice PDF attachments, sending Sunbelt the Palmetto nonprofit-rate invoice and vice versa
- Sunbelt's Raj Greenfield saw the nonprofit pricing, emailed Emeka for clarification, and when Emeka didn't respond clearly, escalated directly to David
- Palmetto's Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged the inflated invoice as an audit risk
- Emeka went quiet for days before eventually reconstructing the error and sending corrective email replies to both clients explaining the billing error, with promises to provide the corrected PDF copies through finance -- but not yet attaching the correct PDFs at this point (corrective emails sent April 22; Slack reconstruction posted April 23) -- note that the QuickBooks invoices (INV-2026-0398 for Sunbelt at $4,800 and INV-2026-0399 for Palmetto at $2,400) were correct all along; the failure was in the outbound email attachment layer, not in billing
- Marcus framed it as revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk (Slack, April 16)
- Marcus put a hard line under the incident and issued cross-functional EOD action items for Finance, Customer Engagement, and Engineering
OE 5: Check the CRM deal records for Sunbelt Venture Partners and Palmetto Community Foundation to see whether the deals reflect the risk from the invoice swap. Use crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries for "Sunbelt" and "Palmetto." The agent should check whether the deal stage or deal notes have been updated to reflect the at-risk status caused by the pricing exposure. Based on universe data, Sunbelt and Palmetto deals may or may not have been updated to reflect this risk -- the agent needs to verify and potentially flag this gap.
OE 6: Check the CRM deal records for Catalina's accounts -- NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, and PivotPoint Labs -- to see if they reflect vendor rate increase risk. Use crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries for "NorthWind," "Canopy," and "PivotPoint." The agent should also check Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics, and other accounts flagged by Mina and Emeka to verify whether those deal records reflect the vendor exposure. The agent should discover that many deal records likely do not reflect the current risk, confirming David's concern.
OE 7: Look up contact details for Catalina Dubois, David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails. The agent needs:
- Catalina's email: catalina.dubois@moveops.com (recipient of the escalation email)
- David's email: david.chen@moveops.com (recipient of the summary email)
- Marcus's and Chloe's details for scheduling the meeting
OE 8: Send an email (via send_email) to Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) from David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) laying out the vendor rate increase situation, the stakes for her three accounts (NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs), and what David needs from her -- specifically her assessment of which of her clients' relocations use Heartland or Swift and how the rate increases affect those accounts. The email should reference that Marcus's vendor strategy memo for Elena will have a gap without her input, and that Mina and Emeka have already responded.
OE 9: Check calendar availability for David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Chloe Vance using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (for the week of April 27-May 2, 2026) to find a suitable time slot. The prompt explicitly requests: "I also need to sit down with Marcus and Chloe sometime next week."
OE 10: Schedule a meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event for an available slot during the week of April 27 – May 2, 2026. The meeting should relate to aligning the vendor response from finance, operations, and customer engagement perspectives. Attendees should include:
- David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com)
- Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com)
- Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com)
OE 11: Send an email (via send_email) from David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) to David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) with a comprehensive summary of which client accounts are exposed to the Heartland/Swift rate increases and why. The prompt explicitly says "email me the summary of which accounts are exposed and why so I can share it with my team." The summary should cover:
- Mina's findings: Vectral Systems and BrightLoop Analytics are most exposed
- Emeka's findings: Mosaic Robotics is exposed
- Catalina's accounts (NorthWind, Canopy, PivotPoint): exposure status based on Airtable relocation data showing which vendors are assigned to their moves
- The Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap situation: current status of resolution, what Emeka communicated, and whether deal records reflect the risk
- The overall context: dual rate increases (Heartland 20% effective July 1 per Jake Loomis's formal notice, Swift 15% immediate base rate increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge per Greg Pallone's pricing update), Alejandro's margin exposure modeling effort (noting inputs across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM do not yet reconcile into a defensible forecast), and the gap in Catalina's reporting
OE 12: Update CRM records for all accounts exposed to risk, using crm_create_engagement (with risk context in the body) or crm_update_deal_stage (with a risk-related stage name). This is an implicit requirement -- David says "half our deal records don't reflect the risk yet" and "if someone touches one of these deals tomorrow they won't know any of this is happening." The agent should update records for:
- All seven accounts exposed to vendor rate increases: Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics, NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs, and GreenStack Energy. Each update should reference the vendor rate exposure affecting that account.
- Both accounts affected by the invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners and Palmetto Community Foundation. Each update should reference the invoice swap incident and pricing exposure.

outcome

✓Run #3The email to Catalina explicitly mentions Swift multiple times, including 'Swift's $500 surcharge ALREADY applied to Raj Patel's in-flight move', 'Nadia is on Swift', and 'confirm Swift will accept supervised loading protocol'.

4 The email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com (via send_email) mentions the impact on NorthWind Technologies.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Catalina has a dedicated section on NorthWind Technologies, describing Raj Patel's Swift move and Emilia Cruz's Heartland move with full context.

✓Run #5The summary email to David states 'Emeka has reconstructed the issue and sent corrected communications to both clients...clarified to Sunbelt that this was not a pricing change or nonprofit program extension,' confirming Emeka communicated to Raj Greenfield/Sunbelt.
✗Run #6The summary email and final response indicate that Emeka has NOT responded to Raj Greenfield — 'no response from Emeka' and 'Raj followed up again asking for clarification — still no response.' The criterion requires evidence that Emeka sent a corrective reply on April 22, but the agent found no such communication and reported the opposite.

42 When checking calendar availability for the meeting with Marcus Thorne and Chloe Vance, the model must pass a date range that covers the week of April 27, 2026 (i.e., start date no later than 2026-04-27 and end date no earlier than 2026-05-01).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The calendar availability check used start_datetime '2026-04-27 00:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-05-01 23:59:59', correctly covering the full week of April 27 through May 1, 2026.
✓Run #3The calendar availability check used start_datetime '2026-04-27 00:00:00' (≤ April 27) and end_datetime '2026-05-01 23:59:59' (≥ May 1), correctly covering the week of April 27, 2026.
✓Run #4The calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to call used start_datetime '2026-04-27 00:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-05-01 23:59:59', which covers the full work week of April 27–May 1, 2026, satisfying the requirement of start ≤ 2026-04-27 and end ≥ 2026-05-01.
✓Run #5The calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to call used start_datetime '2026-04-27 00:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-05-01 23:59:59', which covers the entire week of April 27–May 1, 2026.
✓Run #6The calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to call used start_datetime '2026-04-27 00:00:00' (no later than April 27) and end_datetime '2026-05-02 23:59:59' (no earlier than May 1), correctly spanning the week of April 27, 2026.
